name: Science Centre Singapore, Singapore
concept&appearance: Designed by architect Raymond Woo, the Science Centre Singapore was conceived as a 'spaceship settled on earth'. The building's main block features a hexagonal design with a 4-storey high central void (The Atrium), creating a monumental impression. The structure rests on truncated pyramidal bases and superimposes hexagonal volumes to house the galleries. The design philosophy focused on accessibility, shifting from a collection-based museum to one centered on self-directed interaction and hands-on discovery. The building was carved out of the National Museum of Singapore to be a separate institution in Jurong East, promoting scientific education in a rapidly industrializing nation.
permanent_exhibitions: The Science Centre houses over 850 exhibits across multiple galleries. Key permanent exhibitions include: 1. 'The Mind's Eye', featuring optical illusions and exhibits on perception; 2. 'Professor Crackitt's Light Fantastic Mirror Maze', a life-size labyrinth of mirrors and infinite reflections; 3. 'Phobia2: The Science of Fear', which explores the historical, cultural, and physiological psychology of fear; 4. 'Energy Story', exploring the sources, transformation, and uses of energy from natural cycles to modern applications; 5. 'Earth Alive', focusing on the forces and processes that drive Earth's changes; 6. 'Climate Changed', an exhibition on the state of the climate and individual impact; 7. 'Waterworks', an outdoor hands-on water physics exhibition; 8. 'Ecogarden', a living outdoor laboratory with plants and animals; 9. 'Kinetic Garden', an outdoor scientific garden demonstrating energy forms; 10. 'Butterflies Up-Close', Singapore's first indoor butterfly sanctuary; 11. 'Know Your Poo', a fun exhibition on human waste and sanitation.
central_hall: The main building features a hexagonal central block that is hollow with a 4-storey high void (The Atrium). This central space serves as the main orientation point, with upper floor rooms in the periphery connected by a circular corridor bridge visible in the front facade. It often hosts temporary exhibitions and large-scale demonstrations.
special_effects_theaters: Omni-Theatre: Southeast Asia's first 8K 3D Digital Dome Theatre (formerly an IMAX Dome). It features a massive 23-meter diameter seamless dome screen that is tilted at a 30-degree angle and stretches 5 storeys high, providing an immersive planetarium and movie experience.
science_popularization_activities: The Centre offers a wide range of educational programs including the 'Fire Tornado Show', a 15-minute demonstration recreating a fire vortex in a specially built chamber; the 'Energy Titans Show', featuring Tesla Coil demonstrations with high-voltage lightning arcs; and stargazing sessions at the Observatory (one of the few located next to the Equator). It also hosts 'KidsSTOP', a dedicated children's science centre, and various STEM workshops, science shows, and the 'Young Scientist Badge' programme for students.
